||I found it interesting how Herbert refers to us as he/she. Is that ever explained?||
sounds a translation error
He's just being absolutely certain he doesn't misgender us
what a respectful man i hope he doesn't commit crimes against humanity
Man has standards (generally very fucked up ones)
maybe he also knows that the keeper can change gender for like 1k silver and he's not sure which gender we are rn
of course the society dedicated to modifying people would respect gender changes
Translator forgot to replace it with whatever code they’re using to display he/she according to our choice of the mc
this should be canon
I believe in m/fKeeper
With MTL is an overuse of “that guy”, “KeeperSir” and Jenkin/Murphy/Mason etc getting genderbent
With human translation it’s word typos, missing space between words, occasional repeated words they forgot to delete
And lines becoming hard to understand whenever the original chinese story text gets a bit poetic/complex i assume
And whenever the og chinese text tries to be funny with intentional typos or use the Dissoluted censored text for storytelling MTL 100% butchers it in various hilarious ways
Jenkin suddenly started saying japanese in arc 1 ch 1 and when i checked it was actually supposed to be an intentional typo to show she’s illiterate
But gpt decided she can speak japanese now
She's so illiterate it went into the negatives and overflowed into knowing a completely different language
Yashiro user jenkin canon
Yashiro user
honestly as much as I still have some issues with the story beat of arc 2 I really appreciate the works from our translators
Doresain lines are all charming
and GMurphy chapter, in all its flaws, still a blast to read
really appreciate them <:Cheerup:1400771555939844149>
the localizers are doing their best <:Cheerup:1400771555939844149>
and the translation for chapter 5-6 arc 1 makes me love it more
still the best mini arc of the game
Meanwhile Desecrate still has grammatical errors…

Quick question about Arc 2 chapters 1-2 (i havent started 3), ||is the reason that Keeper is able to survive being beat up multiple times in the course of the two chapters (listed as multiple bones broken, ribs crushed, etc.) because of the body they are using having been given by daffodil? Is it extra resilient? I know that the keeper tells little william that their body is "different" and that they're not going to die any time soon, but i want to understand if thats because of daffodil, or if its because of something i missed||
huh, I would think it's because of ||keeper being a fragment of tohuwabohu, like how in chapters 5 and 6 they lived despite getting hit by all the things that should have killed them?||
Ah, sorry, it seems i should be spoiler tagging things
please do, yes ^-^;; plenty of new people poke their heads in here, I'm sure
Interesting, ||i guess that could be the reason, seems weird that the properties would extend to the new body, or if its even a physical thing||
||maybe it's tied to the soul/consciousness? or then again, during chapter 9 keeper DID say they were becoming/would become a god, maybe that's part of it??||
||doesnt that mean that keeper is in very little threatening situations if they are a god?||

||i know that mutiple portions of the story mainly put stress on mental strain, so i guess thats the main danger for them, then again, arc 2 chapter 2 changes the challenge/danger to body change, rather than pure physical threat||
||so i guess that being in a naturally stronger body doesnt make keeper any less in danger||

||hm, they still need awakeners to fight with them though, no? divinity doesn't necessarily mean indestructiblility or immortality, in morimens, I don't think||
I haven't actually gotten far into arc 2 yet so I can't say much myself, but maybe someone else will have more thoughts
||true, and if im not misunderstanding the story, part of their divinity is being *able* to summon awakeners, regardless of whether they are physically there or not||
||so that makes sense to me then, it might just be a property of their being (as a fragment) that makes them naturally more resilient, while the godhood is separate from that|| thanks for discussing
its also implied that keeper does get enough rest and recovery between chapters, so doll could force them to heal up before breaking their bones again
and given how the keeper insists on their humanity, i dont think divinity plays a big part in their constitution
Yeah, as far as i understand, arc 2 chapter 1 takes course over about a month? Thats still really fast to heal up though
what?
where is this stated?
Id need to find it, they reference time when discussing the lab being recreated

come to think about it, one of the plot threads that never got resolved is the one in ch6 where keeper inexplicably has an abnormal healing rate for major injuries. its mentioned once and never brought up again
perhaps thats the perk of being a fragment?
Found it
nice
since the power of tohu ex machina and dream logic means if keeper dies ||tohu might just wake up immediately like how keeper wakes up if their pov in the psyche deepdives die||
So that would imply ||the world itself is keeping keeper alive?||
Like, they just, ||cant die, since they *are* in a dream and arent *supposed* to die?||
mayhaps? its kinda hard to tell from the inside
then again the events of ch9 are taken as keepers death even by keeper themself, since their body is unconscious and doesn't seem to have any signs of life

The interesting thing ||about the death to me is that theres no dissolution involved, like even though tohuwabohu is dissolution itself, keeper being absorbed into the gate doesnt cause anyone to forget about them||
